title: Helios — N-Body Studio
description: A real-time gravitational N-body studio: Barnes-Hut O(n log n) solver, SEVEN integrators including 4th- AND 6th-order Yoshida symplectic schemes (Yoshida 6 holds energy to ~1e-13, ~40,000× flatter than Yoshida 4 — near machine precision). NEW — GENERAL RELATIVITY: a first post-Newtonian (1PN) correction about the dominant mass, a₁ₚₙ = (μ/c²r³)[(4μ/r−v²)r + 4(r·v)v], wired into every integrator with a momentum-conserving reaction on the central body, so eccentric orbits precess into rosettes — the mechanism behind Mercury's 43″/century perihelion advance. A RELATIVITY LAB integrates a body on a 4th-order Runge-Kutta, detects its periapsis passages and averages the azimuthal advance, then checks the measured precession head-to-head against the closed-form Δϖ = 6πμ/(c²a(1−e²)) per orbit (exact in the weak field, departing by the genuine higher-order PN terms as v/c grows) — and feeds the same formula Mercury's real numbers to recover 42.98″/century. A from-scratch CHAOS LAB integrates the variational (tangent) equations with the exact analytic tidal tensor to compute the maximal Lyapunov exponent (Benettin renormalisation) and the MEGNO indicator ⟨Y⟩ (Cincotta & Simó) — telling a regular orbit (⟨Y⟩→2) from a chaotic one (⟨Y⟩ grows linearly) live, with order/chaos classification and MEGNO/λ-vs-time plots. A SPECTRAL LAB implements Laskar's NAFF (Numerical Analysis of Fundamental Frequencies) on a hand-written FFT — a Hann-windowed correlation refined between FFT bins by golden-section search (super-resolution to ~1e-8 of a bin) — to read an orbit's fundamental frequency and harmonic line spectrum and a frequency-map diffusion chaos index (Laskar 1990), and a POINCARÉ LAB stamps a test particle's surface-of-section in the co-rotating frame of the two heaviest bodies, turning an invariant torus into a closed curve and chaos into a scattered cloud. Plus an osculating-orbit overlay that reconstructs the live Kepler ellipse of any body from its eccentricity vector, a restricted-three-body layer drawing the five Lagrange points and the zero-velocity (Hill-region) curves of the Jacobi integral, inelastic collisions & accretion, a shadow-copy orbit forecaster, live energy/momentum/virial diagnostics, sixteen astrophysical presets (a GR-precession rosette, the figure-eight choreography and its chaotic 'broken eight' twin, the Pythagorean three-body problem, Saturn's rings, Jupiter's Trojans, an eccentric Kepler showcase, co-orbital horseshoes & tadpoles, a hierarchical triple), colour-by speed/mass/acceleration, shareable permalinks, and an in-app 27-check numerical self-test that re-derives every physical claim live — down to a finite-difference proof of the tidal tensor, the time-reversibility of the integrator, FFT round-trips, NAFF sub-bin super-resolution, Jacobi-constant conservation along a Poincaré section, and the relativistic precession matching the closed-form GR formula — all hand-written TypeScript on a Canvas.
